title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Chile
description: Chile's same-sex marriage law, approved by Congress in December 2021, came into force in March 2022, allowing same-sex couples to marry nationwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Switzerland
description: Switzerland's marriage-for-all law, approved by referendum in September 2021, entered into force on 1 July 2022, opening civil marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andorra passes same-sex marriage law
description: Andorra's parliament approved legislation opening marriage to same-sex couples in 2022, with the law scheduled to take effect the following year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United States codifies same-sex marriage in federal law
description: The US Congress passed the Respect for Marriage Act in 2022, writing federal recognition of same-sex and interracial marriages into statute and repealing the Defense of Marriage Act.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: More Japanese prefectures adopt same-sex partnership recognition
description: Several Japanese prefectures, among them Aomori, Akita, Fukuoka, Tochigi and Tokyo, along with additional municipalities, introduced partnership certificate systems giving limited recognition to same-sex couples in 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Antigua and Barbuda decriminalizes same-sex activity
description: The High Court of Antigua and Barbuda struck down colonial-era provisions criminalizing consensual same-sex activity in 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Saint Kitts and Nevis decriminalizes same-sex activity
description: A court ruling in Saint Kitts and Nevis invalidated the laws criminalizing consensual same-sex activity between adults in 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Singapore repeals Section 377A
description: Singapore's parliament repealed Section 377A of the Penal Code in 2022, ending the criminalization of sex between men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Barbados decriminalizes same-sex activity
description: The High Court of Barbados struck down the country's laws against consensual same-sex activity in December 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Spain adopts new anti-discrimination protections
description: Spain enacted anti-discrimination legislation in 2022 covering sexual orientation and gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nevada voters add LGBTQ protections to state constitution
description: Nevada voters approved ballot measure Question 1 with 58 percent support in November 2022, making Nevada the first US state to write sexual orientation and gender identity or expression into its state constitution.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile equalizes the age of consent
description: Chile removed the higher age of consent that had applied to same-sex relations, setting a single age of consent for all couples in 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UN committee rules criminalizing lesbian relationships violates rights
description: In March 2022 the UN Committee on the Elimination of Discrimination against Women held that laws criminalizing consensual same-sex activity between women violate human rights. The case, brought by Sri Lankan activist Rosanna Flamer-Caldera, was the first UN case centered on lesbian and bisexual women.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jowelle de Souza becomes first openly transgender parliamentarian in the Caribbean
description: Jowelle de Souza took a seat in the Senate of Trinidad and Tobago in 2022, making her the first openly transgender member of a parliament anywhere in the Caribbean.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jamie Wallis becomes first openly transgender MP in the House of Commons
description: British MP Jamie Wallis came out as transgender in 2022, the first sitting member of the UK House of Commons to do so openly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Karine Jean-Pierre becomes first openly lesbian White House Press Secretary
description: Karine Jean-Pierre took over as White House Press Secretary in 2022, the first openly lesbian person and the first Black woman to hold the role.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kamala Harris is first sitting US vice president to speak at a Pride festival
description: Vice President Kamala Harris addressed the Capital Pride Festival in Washington, D.C. in 2022, the first sitting US vice president to speak at an LGBTQ+ Pride festival.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Pride Month reception at the US vice president's residence
description: Kamala Harris hosted a Pride Month reception at the vice-presidential residence in 2022, the first such event held there by a sitting US vice president.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly transgender members elected to Brazil's National Congress
description: Duda Salabert and Erika Hilton won seats in Brazil's Chamber of Deputies in the 2022 elections, becoming the first two openly transgender people elected to the National Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First US congressional race between two openly gay candidates
description: The 2022 New York congressional contest between George Santos and Robert Zimmerman was the first US general election for a congressional seat in which both major-party candidates were openly gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: George Santos is first openly gay non-incumbent Republican elected to Congress
description: George Santos won a New York congressional seat in 2022, becoming the first openly gay Republican elected to the US Congress as a non-incumbent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Becca Balint is first openly gay person elected to Congress from Vermont
description: Becca Balint won Vermont's US House seat in 2022, the first openly gay person and the first woman to represent the state in Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Leigh Finke is first openly transgender person elected to Minnesota legislature
description: Leigh Finke won a seat in the Minnesota House in 2022, becoming the state's first openly transgender legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly LGBTQ women elected to the Minnesota state Senate
description: Erin Maye Quade and Clare Oumou Verbeten won Minnesota state Senate seats in 2022, becoming the chamber's first openly LGBTQ women and its first Black women members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: James Roesener is first openly transgender man elected to a US state legislature
description: James Roesener won a New Hampshire state House seat in 2022, the first openly transgender man elected to any state legislature in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alicia Kozlowski is first openly non-binary person elected to Minnesota legislature
description: Alicia Kozlowski won a Minnesota House seat in 2022, becoming the first openly non-binary member of the state's legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: SJ Howell is first openly non-binary person elected to Montana legislature
description: SJ Howell won a seat in the Montana legislature in 2022, the first openly non-binary person elected to that body.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amir Ohana becomes first openly LGBT Speaker of the Knesset
description: Amir Ohana was elected Speaker of Israel's Knesset in 2022, the first openly LGBT person to hold the position.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Davante Lewis is first openly LGBTQ person elected to state office in Louisiana
description: Davante Lewis won election to the Louisiana Public Service Commission in 2022, becoming the state's first openly LGBTQ elected state official.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Corey Jackson is first openly gay Black member of California legislature
description: Corey Jackson won a California Assembly seat in 2022, becoming the first openly gay Black person elected to the state's legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maura Healey elected as first openly lesbian US governor
description: Maura Healey won the Massachusetts governorship in 2022, becoming the state's first openly gay governor and the first openly lesbian governor in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Eric Sorensen is first openly gay man elected to Congress from Illinois
description: Eric Sorensen won an Illinois US House seat in 2022, the first openly gay man to represent the state in Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Erick Russell is first openly gay African American elected statewide in the US
description: Erick Russell won the office of Connecticut state treasurer in 2022, becoming the first openly gay African American elected to statewide office anywhere in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jolanda Jones is first openly gay Black member of Texas legislature
description: Jolanda Jones won a Texas House seat in 2022, becoming the first openly gay Black person elected to the state's legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Openly LGBT candidates run in every US state for the first time
description: The 2022 US general election was the first in which openly LGBT candidates stood for office in all fifty states.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Willow Pill is first openly transgender winner of a regular US Drag Race season
description: Willow Pill won season 14 of RuPaul's Drag Race in 2022, the first openly transgender contestant to take a regular US season of the show.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: L. Morgan Lee is first openly transgender Tony nominee in an acting category
description: L. Morgan Lee received a 2022 Tony nomination for Best Featured Actress in a Musical for her role in A Strange Loop, the first openly transgender performer nominated in a Tony acting category.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ariana DeBose is first openly queer woman of color to win an acting Oscar
description: Ariana DeBose won the 2022 Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress for playing Anita in West Side Story, becoming the first openly queer woman of color and the first Afro-Latina to win an acting Oscar.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Peppa Pig introduces its first same-sex couple
description: The children's series Peppa Pig showed its first same-sex couple in 2022, presenting Penny Polar Bear's two mothers in the episode Families.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bros is first major-studio gay romantic comedy with an all-LGBTQ principal cast
description: The film Bros premiered in 2022 as the first gay romantic comedy from a major Hollywood studio to feature an entirely LGBTQ principal cast.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Molly Kearney joins Saturday Night Live as first out non-binary cast member
description: In September 2022, Molly Kearney was announced as a Saturday Night Live cast member, the first openly non-binary performer in the show's cast.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Unholy tops Billboard Hot 100 with transgender and non-binary artists
description: The single Unholy reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2022, making Kim Petras the first openly transgender woman and Sam Smith the first openly non-binary artist to top the chart.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Amy Schneider wins the Jeopardy! Tournament of Champions
description: Amy Schneider became the first openly transgender contestant to compete in and win the Jeopardy! Tournament of Champions in 2022.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Disney Animation debuts its first openly LGBTQ main character
description: Walt Disney Animation Studios released Strange World in 2022, the studio's first feature with an openly gay main character.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First LGBTQ-led Hallmark Channel Christmas movie premieres
description: The Holiday Sitter premiered on the Hallmark Channel in 2022 as the network's first Christmas movie led by an LGBTQ storyline.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dani Oliva becomes first openly transgender executive at a major music company
description: Dani Oliva, a transgender man, was named Vice President of Legal and Business Affairs at Venice Music in 2022, the first openly transgender executive at a major music company.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Carl Nassib is first openly gay player in an NFL playoff game
description: Carl Nassib played in an NFL playoff game on 15 January 2022, the first openly gay player to appear in the league's postseason.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lia Thomas is first openly transgender NCAA Division I national champion
description: Swimmer Lia Thomas won the women's 500-yard freestyle at the 2022 NCAA championships in 4:33.24, becoming the first openly transgender athlete to win an NCAA Division I national title in any sport.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ryan Resch is first openly gay basketball-operations executive in the NBA
description: Ryan Resch of the Phoenix Suns came out in 2022, becoming the first openly gay person known to work in basketball operations in an NBA front office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Igor Benevenuto is first FIFA-listed referee to come out as gay
description: Brazilian referee Igor Benevenuto came out as gay in 2022, the first referee on FIFA's international list to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Peter Caruth is first Irish men's international hockey player to come out
description: Field hockey international Peter Caruth came out as gay in 2022, a first for a men's international hockey player from Ireland.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nadezhda Karpova is first openly gay Russian national-team athlete
description: Footballer Nadezhda Karpova came out in 2022, becoming the first athlete of a Russian national team to be openly gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Travis Shumake is first openly gay driver in an NHRA national event
description: Travis Shumake competed in a national event on the National Hot Rod Association drag-racing circuit in 2022, the first openly gay driver to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ellia Green becomes first Olympian to come out as a transgender man
description: Australian rugby sevens gold medalist Ellia Green came out in 2022, the first Olympic athlete to come out as a transgender man.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
